name: AMQP Binding
description: Schema describing an AMQP 0-9-1 binding. Bindings are rules that exchanges use to route messages to queues. A binding links a queue to an exchange with a routing key and optional arguments for headers-based matching.

source.description: The name of the exchange from which messages are routed.

destination.description: The name of the queue or exchange to which messages are routed.

destinationType.description: Whether the destination is a queue or an exchange.

routingKey.description: The routing key for the binding. For topic exchanges, supports wildcards: * matches one word, # matches zero or more words.

arguments.description: Optional arguments for the binding. Used primarily with headers exchanges for matching on message header values.

x-match.description: Matching algorithm for headers exchange bindings. 'all' requires all headers to match, 'any' requires at least one.
